anonymized_passage: before staffing a time-limited service window, before customer arrivals and service durations is known, a service-operations manager sets staffing for the window using arrival-rate estimates, service-time estimates, wage cost, and delay penalties. The stated objective is to minimize expected staffing and delay cost, subject to the condition that staff cannot be added after the cutoff in the stated model. Several operational considerations connect the decision to later outcomes, but their relationship is not specified. The decision is important and involves competing considerations that require further analysis. The scope of the resulting claim remains to be established. The study identifies the conditions under which one additional server has the greatest operational value.
